intro: Grastidium quinquedentatum, also known as Paterson's curse, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and as an antiseptic agent, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Grastidium quinquedentatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects.
